request: Tail number of this aircraft.
Seniority years of the crew lead.
Show the third flight in the list, whole record.
Pull the last arrival on the list, entire record.
Keep flights two through four, in listed order.
Does this record include a squawk field? Just true or false.
Gate letter for this flight; report "TBD" if none recorded.
Name the cultivar.
Sun needs from the care sheet.
Show the first plant in the list, full record.
Pull the second-to-last seed lot record, in full.
Keep plants three through five, in listed order.
How many pollinators are listed for this plant?
Does this plant record have a trellis_need field? true or false.
Bloom month, or "unknown" when it isn't recorded.
Resting heart rate for this session.
Coach initials off the coach card.
Show the second workout in the list, whole record.
Keep only the first three workouts, in order.
Does this workout include a cooldown_min field? true or false.
Coach initials, falling back to "staff" when missing.
how many of these vessels are currently moored?
which vessels are carrying grain? give just the vessel names
what is the deepest draught in metres across these vessels?
how many crew members are aboard across all these vessels combined?
which callsigns belong to vessels that need more than two tugs?
a flat 75 handling charge applies to every call — what does each vessel's harbor dues come to with that charge added? give just the updated amounts, in the order listed
which vessel has the shallowest draught? give only its name
which berth numbers hold vessels whose cargo kind mentions 'chilled'?
which vessels have more ballast tons aboard than fuel bunker tons? name the vessels
how many of these showings run past 100 minutes?
which screening halls appear in this schedule? name each hall only once
how many admissions did these showings take in altogether?
which screening halls have a matinee showing?
which picture earned the highest box takings? just the picture's name
what are all the footage figures in feet across these prints, arranged from shortest to longest?
how many places sat empty at each showing — hall places minus admissions? give just the numbers, in the order given
going down the list in order, what's the name of the first picture shot on 70 millimetre gauge?
which artifact numbers are not currently out on loan?
what's the smallest insured value, in thousands, among these pieces?
which gallery wings are represented among these exhibits? name each wing just once
which artifact numbers came from an excavation site whose name mentions 'Delta'?
what's the average footfall across these gallery wings?
which artifact number belongs to the piece acquired in the most recent year?
which artifact numbers are insured for at least 500 thousand?
what is each artifact's mass in grams — kilograms times a thousand? give just the numbers, in the order listed
hey, going through this roast log - can you pull out the bean origin for every batch that scored above 88 on tasting marks? keep them in the same order they show up
we're doing inventory on the fermenters - what do all the batch liters add up to across these entries? just give me the single total number
which roast profiles actually show up in this log? I want each profile listed once only, sorted alphabetically
looking at these beer batches, what's the single highest fermented days figure in here? just that one number please
could you reorder these kegs from the lowest abv percent up to the highest? give me back the complete records, not just the numbers
which blends have aroma notes that mention citrus somewhere in the text? list just the blend labels, in the same order as the log

input.aroma_notes: stone fruit with a citrus zest finish

request: new brews always get appended at the bottom of this list, so can you show me the entire last record - the whole thing, not just one field
turns out the kettle probe reads 3 degrees low - add 3 to every mash heat reading and give me back just those corrected numbers, keeping the log order
what's the finest grind we've dialed in, meaning the smallest grind setting number anywhere in this list? just the number on its own
from tonight's observing log, can you list the constellation for each entry where the apparent magnitude reading is below 4? keep the original log order
can you line these telescopes up from the widest aperture in centimeters down to the narrowest? I want the full records back in that order
for the entries where the seeing condition was exactly 'crisp', which target bodies were we on? list them in the order they were logged
what's the largest apparent magnitude value anywhere in this session - basically the faintest thing we recorded? just give me that number
this log is written in the order we observed things - what's the target body in the very first entry? just that one value
can you convert each exposure from seconds to minutes by dividing by 60, and hand back just those minute numbers in the same order as the log?
pull the complete record for every entry whose log remarks mention 'dew' anywhere in the text, and keep them in the order they were written

input.log_remarks: dew forming on the corrector plate
heavy dew by 2am, packed up early
no dew at all, great transparency

expected_output.log_remarks: dew forming on the corrector plate
heavy dew by 2am, packed up early
no dew at all, great transparency

request: add up the exposure seconds across every entry in the session - I just want the one grand total
can you list just the tail numbers of the planes sitting on a layover longer than 90 minutes? same order as they appear on the board
sort these aircraft from the fewest flight hours to the most, then read me back only the tail numbers in that sorted order
which flight is stuck with the single longest layover in minutes? I want that entire record back, not just the number
what boarding groups are actually in use on this manifest? list each group just once, in ascending order
for each plane, divide the fuel kilograms by the flight hours to get a burn-per-hour figure, and give me just those numbers in the same order as the list
whichever flight is listed last on this board - what gate is it at? just the gate, nothing else
which callsigns got assigned a runway that has the letter L in it? keep them in the order from the strip list
a few of these are overnight red-eyes - can you list the tail numbers for just the ones where the red eye flag is true, in the given order?
Return an object mapping each distinct sun_needs value to the number of plants that have it.
Build an object that maps each cultivar to its bloom_month.
Which plant takes the most days to germinate? Return just that plant's cultivar as a plain string.
List the cultivar names ordered from the earliest bloom_week to the latest.
Across all the nursery yards, list every distinct soil amendment exactly once, sorted alphabetically.
Considering only the potted plants, is at least one of them frost hardy? Return true or false.
Return an object mapping each soil_blend to the list of cultivars planted in it; within each list, keep the cultivars in the same order they appear in the input.
Each key names a garden bed and its value lists the germination day counts observed there; replace each list with its average, leaving the keys unchanged.
Return the same array of plant records in the same order, but with the propagation_notes and intake_batch fields removed from every record.
Return an object mapping each coach_initials value to the average cooldown_min across that coach's workouts.
reference_program: group_by(.coach_initials) | map({key: .[0].coach_initials, value: (map(.cooldown_min) | add / length)}) | from_entries

request: For each coach, pick the workout with that coach's highest burpee_tally; return just the list of those wod_label values, ordered alphabetically by coach_initials.
The keys are class sessions and the values are turnout counts; list the session names whose turnout is at least 12, keeping them in the order they appear.
Looking only at workouts where kettlebell_kg is 24 or more, do all of them have a warmup_min of at least 10? Return true or false.
Return an object with exactly two fields: lowest_rhr set to the athlete name with the smallest resting_hr, and highest_rhr set to the athlete name with the largest resting_hr.
Each key is a training hall and its value lists class turnout counts; replace each list with its single highest turnout, keeping the keys unchanged.
List the wod_label values ordered from the most rounds_finished down to the fewest.
The keys are circuit stations and the values are work intervals in seconds; return the same object but keep only the stations whose interval is at least 60 seconds.
Return an object mapping each cargo_kind to the total crew_aboard summed over the vessels carrying that cargo.
Find the vessel with the shallowest draught_m and return an object containing only its vessel and draught_m fields.
How many different cargo_kind values appear across these vessels? Return a single number.
Is every vessel in this list moored? Return true or false.

The keys are anchorages and the values are how many ships are waiting at each; turn this into an array of objects, each with the fields anchorage and waiting_ships, sorted from the fewest waiting_ships to the most.
Return an object mapping each harbor to the largest tonnage among the vessels in that harbor.
Return an array of objects keeping only the vessel and knots_logged fields of each record, ordered from the highest knots_logged to the lowest.
Count how many voyage legs are listed in total across all the vessels; return a single number.
Total admissions per screening hall — one object per hall with screening_hall and admissions holding that hall's sum.
Count screenings per director — objects with director_initials and screenings giving each director's record count.
For each screening hall keep just its record with the biggest reel_len_min; return those full records.
Titles of the three best-attended pictures, highest admissions first.
Titles of the matinee screenings only, ordered shortest reel_len_min first.
List the halls that appear on more than one record — hall names only, each once.
For each hall, swap its screening list for the sum of those screenings' admissions.
Keys are picture titles, values are admissions — list the titles that drew at least 300.
Strip projector_no and gala_night from every record; keep the rest untouched and in order.
Mean appraisal per wing — objects with gallery_wing and mean_appraisal_k.
Build one object: oldest holds the artifact_no with the smallest acquisition_year, newest the artifact_no with the largest.
Per wing, flag whether any piece sits on loan — objects with gallery_wing and a boolean has_loan.
Keys are wings, values are footfall — keep only the wings with footfall of at least 800.
Every material used across the vitrines, deduplicated.
How many distinct wings house these artifacts? Just the number.
Drop the pieces that are on loan, then remove the on_loan field from the records that remain; keep input order.
Total litres per brew house — objects with brew_house and poured_ltr holding the summed batch_ltr.
Harvest lots ordered shortest to longest fermented_days — lot codes only.
For each bean origin keep just its grind_setting, under the same keys.
Keys are taprooms, values are kegs filled — how many taprooms hit 20 or more?
Is any bottling run dry-hopped? One boolean.
Did every run ferment at least 12 days? One boolean.
List each roast profile once.
One object: biggest_ltr and smallest_ltr — the largest and smallest batch_ltr across the runs.
what number comes after the dash in each target designation? I want them as real numbers, in the order the observations are listed.
each leg chain strings the airports together with '>' — what's the first stop each flight makes after its starting airport? one airport per flight, keeping flight order.
could you give me every cultivar name run together as one line, separated by a comma and a space, in the order they're listed?
expected_output: Moon Ember, Silver Thorn, Dusky Bell, Golden Sprite, Rose Aurora

request: which constellations in this log have names that begin with 'Ly'? keep them in log order.
what does each tail number look like with all the dashes taken out? same order as listed.
what are the cultivar names with the leading 'cv. ' marker stripped off the front? keep the listed order.
how would each observation read as a caption in exactly the form 'Vega (mag 0.03)'? one caption per observation, in order.
what would a CSV row for each flight look like, with the tail number first and the gate letter second? one row per flight, top to bottom.
can you lay each plant out as a tab-separated line — cultivar first, then its germination days — one line per plant, top to bottom?
each azimuth track packs its readings into one string separated by semicolons — what was the last reading of each track, as a number? keep observation order.
taking just the first two characters of each runway, what are the runway numbers as actual numbers? flight order.
how many of these cultivars have 'Rose' somewhere in the name?
the destination codes were keyed in lowercase — what are they all in capitals, keeping flight order?
germination days got stored as text here — what do they sum to, as a number?
looking only at the Cygnus observations, what's the smallest apparent magnitude on record?
which tail numbers sat through a layover longer than 90 minutes? alphabetical order, please.
for the plants that need full sun, what's their average germination time in whole days, rounding down?
what are the three largest azimuth readings in this session, biggest first?
how many different boarding groups appear across these flights?
considering only the potted plants, which cultivar blooms in the earliest month?
how many observations were made on the 200 cm reflector with an exposure longer than 120 seconds?
adding up just the flights headed to KDEN, how many minutes of layover is that in total?
across all the beds, which pests were sighted? name each pest just once — any order is fine.
which sky objects were logged with a moon_phase noted on the record? keep log order.
within boarding group B, which tail number has the longest layover?
every call sign in this list is three chunks glued together with dashes, like ZQ-4471-M — can you pull out just the middle chunk for each ship? one list, same order as the ships appear.
each class code wedges a 4-digit start time between dashes, like MON-0630-CORE — can you grab just those four digits for every class, kept as text, in one list in the order they appear?
can you list the movie titles that contain the letters 'night' anywhere in them? capitalization shouldn't matter, and keep them in the order they show up.
the draught readings came over as text with ' m' stuck on the end — can you strip the unit off each one and hand the values back as plain numbers? one list, same order as given.
can you smoosh all the coach initials into one single string, separated by slashes, going in the order the sessions are listed?
turn each screening into a CSV line for me — the movie title first, then the admissions number — one line per screening, in the given order.
I need a placard string for each ship: the vessel name in all caps, then a space, the word 'berth' in lowercase, a space, and its berth_no. one list, same order as the ships appear.
every locker tag here starts with 'LKR-' — can you chop that prefix off and list what's left for each one, keeping the given order?
can you slugify the hall names? everything lowercase and each space swapped for an underscore — return them as one list in the order given.
each voyage_leg strings the stops together with ' > ' between them — I only want the very last stop for each ship, in one list, same order as given.
give me one tab-separated line per session — the wod_label, then a tab, then the resting_hr — in the order the sessions appear.
each reel_code has a 4-digit year buried in it — can you fish those years out and give them back as actual numbers, one list, same order as listed?

our wod labels squish the word 'Week' down to 'Wk' with the number jammed right after, like 'Wk3' — can you expand each one so it reads 'Week 3' instead, keeping the rest of the label and the list order the same?
for the sessions where resting_hr came in under 60, can you give me their wod labels sorted alphabetically?
how many crew are sitting in port right now? add up crew_aboard across just the ships that are moored — I want the one total.
looking only at the matinee showings, which screening_hall pulled the biggest admissions number? just that one hall's name, nothing else.
what's the average cooldown_min across all these sessions, rounded down to a whole number?
which cargo kinds are moving on ships drawing more than 9 meters? give each cargo_kind once — order doesn't matter to me.
quick count for me: how many of these showings run longer than 120 minutes in reel_len_min and are not matinees? just the number.
sort the sessions from the lowest resting_hr up and hand me just the wod labels of the two lowest ones, lowest first.
for the ships with at least 20 crew_aboard, can you give me a small object for each holding just the vessel and the berth_no? collect them into one list in the order the ships are listed.
rank these showings by admissions, biggest first, and just give me the screening_hall names in that order.
among the sessions where resting_hr was 60 or higher, which one had the shortest cooldown_min? just give me its wod_label.
for just the moored ships, convert each draught_m to centimeters — multiply by 100 — and round to the nearest whole number. one list, same order as the ships appear.
can you total up the admissions for everything that played in the Rialto Annex? just the one number.
Multiply together the yield_factor values of every batch and return that single product as one number.
Return an object mapping each gallery_wing to the number of artifact records in that wing.
For each observation multiply its exposure_sec by its frame_reps, then return the grand total of all those products as a single number.
Return an object mapping each roast_profile to the total batch_kg roasted under that profile.
Add up every number inside the sub_exposures arrays across all sessions and return the single grand total.
Add up insured_value_k across only the artifacts whose on_loan is false, and return that single total number.

Groups in this collection can nest inside each other through their holdings arrays. Gather every artifact_no that appears at any depth into one flat list, keeping the order they appear in the document.
Round every number anywhere in this nested brew document down to the nearest whole number, leaving all structure, strings, and key names unchanged.
reference_program: walk(if type == "number" then floor else . end)

request: azimuth_deg values appear at several different depths in this observing plan; add every one of them together and return the single total.
Remove the curator_initials field from every object at every level of nesting, and return the structure otherwise unchanged.
reference_program: walk(if type == "object" then del(.curator_initials) else . end)

request: fetch_route holds a sequence of keys pointing to a spot inside cellar_tree. Starting at cellar_tree, follow that route and return the value found there.

Find every place the exact string 'Obsidian Mask' occurs anywhere in this document, and return a list of the paths to each occurrence — every path given as an array of keys and array positions — in document order.
Delete exactly two entries: gear_slack inside mount_cfg, and night_log inside observer_meta. Return the whole document with everything else intact.
For each observation in order, produce the string 'naked_eye' when its magnitude_app is strictly below 4, and 'scope_only' otherwise; return the resulting list of labels.
Classify this cask by its fermented_days: strictly more than 30 gives 'cellar_ready', 10 through 30 (inclusive) gives 'conditioning', and below 10 gives 'still_young'. Return just the label string.
reference_program: if .fermented_days > 30 then "cellar_ready" elif .fermented_days >= 10 then "conditioning" else "still_young" end

request: For each artifact in order: when on_loan is true output its borrower_org, otherwise output its display_case. Return the list of those values.
Convert each pointing's azimuth_deg to a signed value: subtract 360 when it is strictly above 180, otherwise keep it as-is. Return the list of numbers in the original order.
Go through the batches in order and, after each one, record the cumulative batch_ltr so far. Return the list of running totals, one entry per batch.
Scanning the observations in order, after each one record the lowest magnitude_app seen so far. Return that list of running minimums, one entry per observation.
